title: Solar Energy Budget
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive Sankey diagram showing how solar energy flows from the sun through the atmosphere to producers, with adjustable cloud cover

learningObjectives: Trace the path of solar energy from the sun through the atmosphere to producers
Explain why only a small fraction of solar energy is captured by photosynthesis
Analyze how cloud cover affects the energy budget and ecosystem productivity

keyQuestions: What percentage of solar energy actually enters ecosystems through photosynthesis?
Where does most of the sun's energy go before it can reach producers?
How does cloud cover affect the amount of energy available to ecosystems?
Why does Earth's energy budget limit the length of food chains?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think most sunlight is used by plants, when actually less than 1% is captured by photosynthesis
Students may confuse energy reflected back to space with energy absorbed by the atmosphere
Students sometimes believe clouds only block sunlight rather than also reflecting energy
assessmentOpportunities: Have students calculate energy available at each trophic level starting from the solar budget
Ask students to predict ecosystem productivity changes under different cloud cover scenarios
Students create their own Sankey diagrams for a local ecosystem's energy flow
